ref: When the chief priests and the Pharisees heard these stories, they knew Jesus was talking about them.
text: Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm, ni Farasi­fɔm n tiiri miɛɛnu n bo Yesu boori i sɔ ni, na ba fa nu ka i boori i niiri bɛrɛ wɔ.
translated: When the disciples were gathered together, and the Pharisees and the crowds were seeking him, and they heard that he was there, they came to him.

ref: Come and set up your kingdom, so that everyone on earth will obey you, as you are obeyed in heaven.
text: Ma u fɛmɛya n bra. Ma u koroo­wa yo asiɛŋgu fɛn, ka alecɛɛma nu.
translated: They are going to the farm. They will harvest the corn today, and then they will rest.

ref: Salt is good. But if it no longer tastes like salt, how can it be made salty again? Have salt among you and live at peace with each other.
text: Ya si ka njin yo fiɛ, ama, nzɛn i fiɛfiɛ nya ba saari wieeri, i koro yo má fiɛfiɛ kun. Am ma am wo yo fiɛ ka njin am beŋgu nu, na am wo ni tanalaifiɛ.”
translated: If you want to learn, my child, you must be patient and listen carefully, because learning is not a simple matter. But if you are willing to learn and listen, I will teach you.

ref: Then Jesus spoke sternly to the demon. It went out of the boy, and right then he was healed.
text: Na Yesu atiɛn ajini n ŋu, na wɔ fite ayaki baa ni. Dika n nu bɛrɛ, na baa n anya laifiɛ.
translated: And Jesus said to them, and they found a donkey tied there. When he untied it, the donkey's owners said to them.

ref: when four people came up, carrying a man on a mat because he could not walk.
text: I wo bu kere su ni, na minɛ nbem aba i dɔ, bu nu minɛ nna sɔ sɔnɔ bo i saam ni i jaam awu.
translated: When I go to the farm, my mother cooks food for me to take, and when I return from the farm, she cooks again for me to eat before I go to sleep.
